title: PRACTICE: Decarbonization Goals and a Climate Future
description: This exercise checks your understanding of the role of climate goals in driving decarbonization and what our future might look like as climate change continues to impact our lives. Let's see how well you understand the importance of setting these goals and how they’ll shape our health, jobs, and the systems we rely on.
topic_path: PRACTICE: Decarbonization Goals and a Climate Future

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the role of international climate goals?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They create a framework for setting goals at a smaller scale.
They create and manage policies with the force of law.
They help plan infrastructure and public work projects.
They fund research and development.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What was the significance of the Paris Climate Agreement?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It set binding international climate goals for the first time.
It was the first international conversation about climate change.
It was the first conference to discuss renewable energy.
It created a detailed plan for climate action in each country.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which international body is responsible for creating the Paris Agreement?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: United Nations (UN)
Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC)
World Health Organization (WHO)
World Trade Organization (WTO)

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is a downside of international climate agreements like the Paris Agreement?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They are difficult to enforce.
They do not include enough countries.
They do not have enough funding for developing climate-friendly technologies.
They do not respond to local climate change impacts.

assessment_items.question_content:
**How do national governments contribute to achieving climate goals set by international agreements?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: By creating and enforcing detailed policies.
By enforcing compliance in other countries.
By coordinating with other countries to determine whether climate change is happening.
By developing commercial products that are climate-friendly.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What role do local governments play in climate change mitigation in the United States?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They regulate building codes and public transportation.
They set vehicle emissions standards.
They have limited to no role.
They organize conservation and restoration projects to protect local ecosystems.
A clear, measurable commitment by a government or other organization to limit the effects of climate change.
Something established as a model or expected rule of measure by an authority such as a government.
A detailed plan to address climate change mitigation and adaptation in a local community.
An effort taken to respond to existing impacts of climate change.

assessment_items.question_content:
**How might climate change impact human health?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It may increase the spread of diseases as warmer temperatures rise.
It may cause species like bats and ticks to go extinct.
It may help food crops grow more abundantly and improve nutrition.
It may make regions more temperate and habitable.

assessment_items.question_content:
**In the last century, deaths from natural disasters have fallen by more than $75\%$ relative to the global population. What is the best explanation for this change?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: We have better warning systems and more resilient infrastructure.
There are fewer natural disasters and extreme weather events.
There is lower population density in urban areas.
There has been large-scale migration away from the coasts.

assessment_items.question_content:
**How will our response to climate change transform our energy systems?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It will increase our production of zero-carbon electricity through renewable sources.
It will decrease our research and development of nuclear energy.
It will decrease electrification of existing fossil-fuel-based systems.
It will increase our mining and use of fossil fuels.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What’s one way climate change will impact human migration?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Sea-level rise will displace millions of people from their homes.
Climate-related migration will push people away from urban areas.
People will migrate only within the borders of their country.
People will relocate from inland areas to coastal areas.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What are some likely economic impacts of climate change?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: More job opportunities in energy research and development
Increased income for small-holder farmers
Fewer business opportunities
Less inequality between communities
